name: Bottom view of BT
category: Binary Trees [Traversals, Medium and Hard Problems]
statement:
Given root of binary tree, return the bottom view of the binary tree.


The bottom view of a binary tree is the set of nodes visible when the tree is viewed from the bottom. Return nodes from the leftmost node to the rightmost node. Also if 2 nodes are outside the shadow of the tree and are at the same position then consider the node that appears later in level traversal.

examples.explanation: From left to right the path is as follows : First we encounter node with value 5. Then we have nodes 8 , 10 but from bottom only 10 will be visible. Next we have 20 , 3 but from bottom only 3 will be visible. Next we have 14 , 22 but from bottom only 14 will be visible. Then we encounter node with value 25.

From left to right the path is as follows : First we encounter node with value 5. Then we have nodes 8 , 10 but from bottom only 10 will be visible. Next we have 20 , 3 and 4. The 3 and 4 will be nodes visible from bottom but as the node 4 appears later from left to right , so only node 4 will be considered visible. Next we have 14 , 22 but from bottom only 14 will be visible. Then we encounter node with value 25.
